key_topics: The Number System: irrational numbers, approximating irrational numbers
Properties of integer exponents, scientific notation
Expressions and Equations: solving linear equations in one variable (one solution, no solution, infinitely many solutions)
Solving pairs of simultaneous linear equations (graphically and algebraically)
Functions: defining functions, function notation, input/output tables
Linear functions: rate of change (slope), y-intercept, slope-intercept form y = mx + b
Comparing linear and non-linear functions
Geometry: Pythagorean theorem and its converse
Applying Pythagorean theorem to find distance in coordinate plane
Congruence and similarity: transformations (translation, rotation, reflection, dilation)
Angle relationships with parallel lines and transversals
Volume of cones, cylinders, and spheres
Statistics and Probability: scatter plots, lines of best fit
Bivariate categorical data, two-way tables
competences: Solve linear equations with rational coefficients
Solve systems of two linear equations by substitution and elimination
Understand and represent functions, identify linear vs. non-linear
Apply the Pythagorean theorem to real-world and geometric problems
Describe transformations and their effects on congruence and similarity
typical_mistakes: Errors solving equations with variables on both sides
Confusing slope formula (rise over run, not run over rise)
Using Pythagorean theorem on non-right triangles

key_topics: Reading Literature: analyzing how themes develop across multiple texts
Analyzing modern fiction and literary non-fiction
Comparing an author's book to a film or play adaptation
Reading Informational Text: delineating and evaluating arguments
Analyzing conflicting information from different sources
Understanding how authors acknowledge and respond to counterarguments
Vocabulary: technical and academic vocabulary, tone, and style
Writing: argumentative essays with sophisticated reasoning and counterarguments
Informative/explanatory writing: sustained multi-paragraph essays
Narrative writing: published-quality narrative with effective technique
Research: long-term research projects, integrating and evaluating sources
Language: verbals (gerunds, participles, infinitives), active vs. passive voice, mood
competences: Write sophisticated multi-paragraph argumentative and informative essays
Analyze complex literary and non-fiction texts with close reading
Conduct and present research using multiple credible sources
Demonstrate mastery of grade 8 grammar including verbals and voice
typical_mistakes: Not integrating quotes smoothly (dropping quotes without context)
Weak paragraph organization (PEEL or TREE structure not followed)
Errors with dangling or misplaced participial phrases

key_topics: Forces and motion: Newton's Laws applied to complex systems
Gravity and gravitational force across distances
Waves: mechanical and electromagnetic wave properties, applications
Information transfer: digital encoding of information
Heredity: DNA structure, mutations, genetic engineering basics
Natural selection: evidence from fossil record, comparative anatomy, DNA
Macroevolution: speciation and extinction events
Earth's history: plate tectonics, rock cycle
Climate systems: causes and evidence of climate change, mitigation strategies
competences: Apply Newton's Laws to multi-step force problems
Explain heredity at molecular level (DNA to traits)
Construct and evaluate evidence for evolution
Analyze climate data and propose evidence-based solutions
typical_mistakes: Thinking organisms evolve by choice or intention
Confusing DNA, genes, and chromosomes as the same thing

key_topics: American history: 20th century — WWI, WWII
The Great Depression: causes, New Deal, social impact
Civil Rights Movement: key events, leaders, legislation
Cold War: US-Soviet tensions, key events (Korean War, Vietnam War, Cuban Missile Crisis)
Post-Cold War world: globalization, terrorism, 9/11
Government and civics: three branches, checks and balances, landmark Supreme Court cases
Economics: GDP, inflation, fiscal and monetary policy
Contemporary global issues: climate, inequality, technology
competences: Analyze the causes and consequences of WWI and WWII
Evaluate the effectiveness of the Civil Rights Movement
Explain the structure of the US government and checks and balances
Analyze contemporary global issues using geographic and economic data
typical_mistakes: Oversimplifying WWII causes (only Hitler, not broader factors)
Not understanding checks and balances as a system
Confusing fiscal policy (government) with monetary policy (Federal Reserve)
